slides.title: Inventions and Creativity
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 4: Inventions and Creativity. Welcome to Unit 4! This unit is all about inventions and creativity — two powerful forces that have shaped the world we live in today. Throughout history, people have looked at problems around them and imagined new ways to solve them. From the simple wheel to the complex machines of the modern age, every invention began as an idea in somebody’s mind. In this unit, you will explore how inventions work, why they matter, and how ordinary people — including young people just like you — have changed the world with creative thinking. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use vocabulary related to inventions, exploration, science, and technology confidently in speech and writing
Read and understand an informational passage about inventions and answer comprehension questions about it
Identify the past simple tense in sentences and explain when and why we use it
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use vocabulary related to inventions, exploration, science, and technology confidently in speech and writing. you will also read and understand an informational passage about inventions and answer comprehension questions about it. you will also identify the past simple tense in sentences and explain when and why we use it. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: straits, explorer, excursion, caravan, territory, navigation. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Inventions and Creativity: source text 1 and Inventions and Creativity: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Past Simple Tense (Reviewed)
bullets: What it means and why it matters The past simple tense is used to talk about actions or events that happened in the past and are now finished. It tells us...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Past Simple Tense (Reviewed). What it means and why it matters The past simple tense is used to talk about actions or events that happened in the past and are now finished. It tells us that something occurred at a specific time and is complete. For example, “The explorer claimed a new route” tells us this action happened in the past. Knowing how to use the past simple helps us share stories, describe experiences, write about history, and communicate about things that have already happened. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
